entry.title: [SERVED] Mary Carillo Reveals What Made Roger Federer So Different
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick sits down with legendary broadcaster Mary Carillo to celebrate Roger Federer's Hall of Fame induction, sharing unforgettable stories from covering his career and what made him unlike any player before or since. Alongside Jon Wertheim, they compare Federer to Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic, reflect on the art of describing greatness, and reveal why Roger's brilliance was as effortless off the court as it was on it.
Check out the Served Merch! https://store.servedpodcast.com/
🎾 Join the Served Chucker's Club for Exclusive Perks: youtube.com/channel/UC0k_--YLuTNuDvq1Dw4zHmw/join

Served with Andy Roddick is your home for all the ATP and WTA Tours Tennis Breaking News, Draw Previews, Match Recaps, Player Interviews, Match Previews and more. We cover the largest tournaments throughout the season including the Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, US Open, Shanghai and talk about the best players including Novak Djokovic, Carlos Alcaraz, Jannik Sinner, Rafael Nadal, Coco Gauff, Iga Świątek, Naomi Osaka, Aryna Sabalenka, Alexander Zverev, Daniil Medvedev, Emma Raducanu, Stefanos Tsitsipas, Roger Federer, Jack Draper.

entry.title: [SERVED] Jake Johnson on Tennis vs. Pickleball & Andy Roddick's Hidden Acting Talent | Q&Andy
entry.summary:
The Dink streams globally on Apple TV on Friday, July 24, 2026
Want to be featured on the next Q&Andy? Send us a video on our socials or email us at askandy@servedmediagroup.com Andy Roddick sits down with actor Jake Johnson ahead of the release of The Dink, the Apple TV pickleball comedy that also marks Roddick's film debut, and the two swap stories about Ed Harris's on set intensity, Brad Gilbert's coaching cameo and emoji habit, and the eternal tennis versus pickleball debate.
📱 Stay up to date with Q&Andy! Follow us on our socials: https://www.instagram.com/servedpodcast https://www.tiktok.com/@served_podcast https://x.com/served_podcast
⏰ TIMESTAMPS: 0:00 – Welcome to Q&Andy with Jake Johnson 1:21 – Casting Andy in The Dink 2:44 – Jake's method prep: training with pro pickleball players 3:04 – The tennis world's obsession with legs 4:56 – Brad Gilbert's "sturdy" legs verdict 5:16 – Andy's injury and most embarrassing moment on set 6:40 – What The Dink is actually about 8:12 – Is Ed Harris terrifying in real life? 9:56 – Jake's Andy Roddick impression 11:09 – Aaron Chen is a comedic genius 13:43 – Tennis vs. pickleball? 15:41 – Brad Gilbert's insane emoji texting habit 16:37 – Which New Girl character would win a Grand Slam first? 17:14 – Random Question of the Day: Chicago travel recs
Thank you to our sponsors: Airbnb

entry.title: [SERVED] Kim Clijsters on Her Biggest Tennis Advice, WTA Eligibility Test & More | Love All w/ Kim Clijsters
entry.summary:
Join 4-time Grand Slam champion Kim Clijsters and tennis reporter Blair Henley as they break down the WTA's new eligibility policy, Stefanos Tsitsipas' resurgence, Paula Badosa's comeback, and Novak Djokovic's latest off-court moves. Plus, Kim shares what the US Open swing is really like, her toughest training secrets, and the best tennis advice she ever received.
Welcome to Love All! If you want to hang out with us behind the scenes follow us on all of our socials: https://www.instagram.com/loveallpodcast/ https://www.tiktok.com/@loveallpodcast https://x.com/loveallpodcast
Blair’s Rec: Lucite Bird Feeder
⏰ TIMESTAMPS: 0:00 Welcome to Love All 3:19 Kim's Comeback: First run since her Achilles rupture 5:31 Tomorrowland 8:30 Henley's Headlines 12:11 Tsitsipas' New Coach 14:19 Summer Break Roundup: Alcaraz's wrist and life off tour 18:24 Djokovic Goes Viral at Fanatics Fest 22:02 Wedding Season: Kasatkina, Paul, & More 25:12 What Kim Would Ask Novak About Biohacking 26:50 WTA's New Genetic Testing Policy 30:14 Kimformation 33:55 Kim's Sauna Secret for Beating the Heat 36:35 Kim's Best Tennis Advice 39:29 Blair's Dad on Why Wrist Instruction Ruins Rec Players 45:55 Rec Room: Lucite Bird Feeders 50:35 Closing Thoughts

entry.title: [SERVED] Mary Carillo talks Hall of Fame, Roger Federer, Becoming a Broadcaster & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim talk to Mary Carillo ahead of her well-deserved International Tennis Hall of Fame induction in Newport (alongside one Roger Federer), Mary opens up on 45 years in sports media, 17 Olympics, six Emmys, and the unlikely night at Madison Square Garden that launched it all.
In this episode:
- The Racket Rundown: Rublev, Tsitsipas, Krejčíková, & Merida Titles - The ATP's plan to unify tennis balls by 2029 - Alcaraz's wrist brace at the 2026 World Cup - Mary Carillo on getting into broadcasting - Her legendary storytelling craft and personalizing athletes for the audience - The day lightning struck the broadcast booth while she was inside
Mary's Badminton rant: https://www.nbcnews.com/video/mary-carillo-s-badminton-rant-remains-the-best-sports-commentary-of-all-time-747874883720
COMMENT BELOW: What is your favorite Mary Carillo moment!?

Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim talk to Mary Carillo ahead of her well-deserved International Tennis Hall of Fame induction in Newport (alongside one Roger Federer), Mary opens up on 45 years in sports media, 17 Olympics, six Emmys, and the unlikely night at Madison Square Garden that launched it all.

In this episode:

- The Racket Rundown: Rublev, Tsitsipas, Krejčíková, & Merida Titles
- The ATP's plan to unify tennis balls by 2029
- Alcaraz's wrist brace at the 2026 World Cup
- Mary Carillo on getting into broadcasting
- Her legendary storytelling craft and personalizing athletes for the audience
- The day lightning struck the broadcast booth while she was inside

entry.title: [SERVED] Sinner's 5th Slam, 100th Major Win, and the Case for an All-Time Great
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim explain why Jannik Sinner is already entering historic territory after another dominant Wimbledon title, discuss the explosive rise of Alexandra Eala and the global fan movement she's creating, and make the case for why every tennis fan needs to watch wheelchair superstar Tokito Oda. Plus, they break down Sinner's incredible statistics, his place among the all-time greats, and why the future of tennis has never looked brighter.

entry.title: [SERVED] US Open Favorites, Coco Gauff's Next Step & Linda Nosková's Tearful Wimbledon Title
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim break down Linda Nosková's emotional Wimbledon championship, Coco Gauff's biggest breakthrough on grass, and why Jannik Sinner enters the US Open as the overwhelming favorite. They also discuss Alexander Zverev's best Wimbledon yet, the wide-open WTA race, and what these results mean for the rest of the 2026 season. entry.title: [SERVED] Serena's Comeback Continues, Arthur Fery Shocks Wimbledon & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim react to Serena Williams' highly anticipated return to tennis, explain why her comeback is far from over, and what it could mean for the US Open. They also break down Grigor Dimitrov's redemption run, Arthur Fery's breakout Wimbledon performance, and the feel-good stories that made this year's Championships unforgettable.

entry.title: [SERVED] Why Do Players Double Fault More On Grass? | Q&Andy
entry.summary:
Want to be featured on the next Q&Andy? Send us a video on our socials or email us at askandy@servedmediagroup.com Andy Roddick answers some of the biggest tennis questions coming out of Wimbledon, from why serve-and-volley has nearly disappeared to whether modern players are simply getting too big, fast, and athletic for the old-school game. He also breaks down Coco Gauff's unique tactical brilliance, explains why players take more risks on grass, shares behind-the-scenes stories about playing on Centre Court, and reflects on the mindset that fueled his career.
📱 Stay up to date with Q&Andy! Follow us on our socials: https://www.instagram.com/servedpodcast https://www.tiktok.com/@served_podcast https://x.com/served_podcast

entry.title: [SERVED] Sabalenka & Swiatek's Grass Court Struggles, Gauff's Drop Shot & More | Love All w/ Kim Clijsters
entry.summary:
Join 4-time Grand Slam champion Kim Clijsters and tennis reporter Blair Henley as they recap Wimbledon, celebrate champions Jannik Sinner and Linda Nosková, and explore why Czech tennis continues to produce Grand Slam winners. Kim also shares behind-the-scenes stories from the Wimbledon Champions' Ball, returning to the All England Club, and experiencing the tournament from a new perspective after retirement. They also break down the WTA Finals move to Indian Wells, the ATP's proposed doubles overhaul, Roger Federer's upcoming Hall of Fame induction, Tatjana Maria's grass-court success, and Kim's tactical analysis of Aryna Sabalenka and Iga Świątek. Plus, hear why Kim believes Czech coaching remains one of the biggest keys to sustained success in tennis.
Welcome to Love All! If you want to hang out with us behind the scenes follow us on all of our socials: https://www.instagram.com/loveallpodcast/ https://www.tiktok.com/@loveallpodcast https://x.com/loveallpodcast

Blair’s Rec: https://www.sephora.com/product/cay-skin-isle-lip-balm-spf-30-P481367?skuId=2561868

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 Wimbledon Recap: Serena's Return, Sinner's Historic Win & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim wrap up the 2026 Wimbledon Championships by breaking down the tournament's biggest stories, from Serena Williams' long-awaited return to Jannik Sinner cementing himself as the game's dominant force with another Grand Slam title. They discuss Sinner's historic run, Linda Nosková's emotional championship, Arthur Fery's breakout, Grigor Dimitrov's redemption, Coco Gauff's grass-court breakthrough, the American men's disappointing results, and why the US Open feels more wide open than ever. Plus, they spotlight Tokito Oda, Jordan Lee's junior title, and the next generation shaping tennis' future.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Wimbledon 2026?

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON WOMEN'S FINAL RECAP: Nosková's Wild Comeback, Sinner vs Zverev Preview & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim react to one of the most emotional Wimbledon women's finals in recent memory, breaking down Linda Nosková's remarkable mental recovery after letting five championship points slip away before capturing her first Grand Slam title. They also explore the Czech Republic's remarkable tennis pipeline, debate whether runner-up speeches should remain mandatory, and preview the men's final with a deep tactical breakdown of Jannik Sinner vs. Alexander Zverev, including why grass could finally change their rivalry.

entry.title: [SERVED] Is This Wilson Racket a CHEAT CODE for Any Player?
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick thought he'd never hit a backhand like this again -- until Wilson's Ron Rocchi walked in with a prototype he wasn't even allowed to keep.
This is a rare, up-close look at a Hall of Fame player hitting with a brand-new racket for the very first time... and absolutely loving it. Watch Andy Roddick and Ron Rocchi, Global Tour Manager at Wilson, break down the technology behind @WilsonRacquet's newest frame -- why the game has changed with fluffing balls, slower courts, and bigger, stronger players, and why half of Wilson's tour athletes asked for an equipment change last season.
Then Andy takes the new racket -- built around a bigger, more uniform sweet spot and next-level torsional stability -- out on court, and the reaction speaks for itself. Plus the pros already switching to it, and the one frame Ron refused to let Andy walk away with.
COMMENT BELOW: What racket are you playing with right now -- and are you due for an upgrade?
Check out Wilson's incredible line of rackets here: https://www.wilson.com/en-us/tennis

entry.title: [SERVED] Can Karolína Muchová Win Wimbledon? Coco Gauff's Heartbreak & More | 2026 Wimbledon Day 11 Recap
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim break down Day 11 of Wimbledon, analyzing Coco Gauff's painful semifinal loss to Karolína Muchová, previewing the blockbuster Novak Djokovic vs. Jannik Sinner showdown, and debating whether Arthur Fery's dream run can continue against Alexander Zverev.
They reveal why Gauff's infamous drop shot wasn't necessarily the wrong decision, explain the tactical chess match that could decide the men's semifinals, dive into Muchová's all-court brilliance, discuss Wimbledon court conditions, and share behind-the-scenes stories from life inside the Championships.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 11?

entry.title: [SERVED] Marta Kostyuk on Ukraine's Resilience & Her Breakthrough Season
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim sat down with Marta Kostyuk to discuss the breakthrough season that's taken her to the top of the game. Kostyuk opened up about the mindset shift behind her consistency, why working with coach Sandra Zaniewska changed everything, the difficult decision to skip Rome before Roland Garros, and what it means to represent Ukraine on the biggest stages in tennis while her family remains back home.
This episode was released on May 19, 2026.
To watch the full episode, click here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=01rNgQjNRJ0

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 Wimbledon Day 9 Recap: Djokovic's Marathon Win, Coco Gauff's Redemption & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick reacts to Day 9 of Wimbledon, breaking down Novak Djokovic's record-setting five-hour epic against Félix Auger-Aliassime, Coco Gauff's statement victory over Jessica Pegula, and why Karolína Muchová may be the most complete player left in the women's draw. Andy also shares what it was like calling Djokovic's historic match for ESPN, previews Taylor Fritz vs. Alexander Zverev, explains why Jannik Sinner's dominance is becoming almost impossible to appreciate in real time, and discusses which players now have the clearest path to lifting the Wimbledon trophy.
COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 9?

They also discuss Jasmine Paolini's resurgence, the evolving women's draw, Grigor Dimitrov's comeback, and who looks best positioned to win Wimbledon.

COMMENT BELOW: What was your favorite match of Day 8?

entry.title: [SERVED] Alex Eala talks Filipino Pride & Handling The Pressure of Fame
entry.summary:
Alex Eala joins Andy Roddick and Jon Wertheim to talk about her tennis journey so far. Eala shares why Filipinos have a one-of-a-kind sense of community, compares the energy to Manny Pacquiao fight nights, and gets honest about balancing fame with the daily grind of competing at the highest level. Alex reveals which past legends she'd love to have faced (Serena? Sharapova?), the one player she's desperate to match up against on tour right now, and the specific parts of her game she's locked in on improving.
This episode was released on March 24, 2026.
Check out the full interview here: https://youtu.be/U1rcV6AlsQo

entry.title: [SERVED] 2026 WIMBLEDON DAY 5 RECAP: Djokovic Ties Federer's Wimbledon Wins Record, Gauff's Grind & More
entry.summary:
Andy Roddick and JW break down Wimbledon Day 5, from Novak Djokovic tying Roger Federer's all-time Wimbledon win record to Naomi Osaka's dominant surge and Jenson Brooksby's inspiring run through the draw. They dig into Jan-Lennard Struff's upset of Daniil Medvedev, preview a red-hot Sabalenka-Osaka showdown, and unpack Coco Gauff's grinding win.
